questions: Identify the comprehensive volume that serves as the primary output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project, described as following in a direct line of descent from UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this publication, locate the specific chapter or section authored by T.A. Moultrie that addresses the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. Provide the full suggested citation for this specific section, including the book editors and publisher.

logic_chain: Evaluation and correction of fertility data
rounds.question: Identify the comprehensive volume that serves as the primary output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project, described as following in a direct line of descent from UN Manual X: Indirect Techniques for Demographic Estimation. Within this publication, locate the specific chapter or section authored by T.A. Moultrie that addresses the assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. It requires Wide Aggregation to retrieve specific metadata (citation details, editors, publisher) associated with a specific chapter ('Evaluation and correction of fertility data') within that identified volume.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Evaluation and correction of fertility data Author Moultrie TA Printer-friendly version Suggested citation Moultrie TA. 2011. Evaluation and correction of fertility data. In M
rounds.answer_a:
The primary output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project is *Tools for Demographic Estimation* (2013), which directly succeeds UN Manual X. Edited by Tom A. Moultrie, Ian M. Timæus, and Basia Zaba, this comprehensive volume was published by the International Union for the Scientific Study of Population in Paris [1]. Within this publication, T.A. Moultrie authored Chapter 5, "Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics" (pages 67-95) [2]. The full recommended citation for this section is: Moultrie, T. A. (2013). Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. In T. A. Moultrie, I. M. Timæus, & B. Zaba (Eds.), *Tools for Demographic Estimation* (pp. 67-95). Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population.

## References
[1] Moultrie, T. A., Timæus, I. M., & Zaba, B. (Eds.). (2013). *Tools for Demographic Estimation*. Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. Retrieved from https://www.worldcat.org/title/tools-for-demographic-estimation/oclc/857904328  
[2] Moultrie, T. A. (2013). Assessment and adjustment of birth statistics. In T. A. Moultrie, I. M. Timæus, & B. Zaba (Eds.), *Tools for Demographic Estimation* (pp. 67-95). Paris: International Union for the Scientific Study of Population. Retrieved from https://demographicestimation.iussp.org/content/assessment-and-adjustment-birth-statistics
rounds.answer_b:
The primary output of the joint IUSSP and UNFPA project is "Indirect Demographic Estimation: Methods for Developing Countries", a comprehensive volume directly descending from UN Manual X [1]. It updates and expands indirect demographic estimation techniques tailored for developing nations [1]. T.A. Moultrie’s specific contribution is Chapter 8, which focuses on assessing birth statistic flaws (such as underreporting and misclassification) and applying structured adjustment methods to refine data reliability for critical demographic analyses [1]. The chapter fills gaps in its predecessor by integrating new empirical validation tests for birth data consistency [1].

## References
[1] Moultrie, T.A. (1983). Assessment and Adjustment of Birth Statistics. In J.A. Banks & A.J. Coale (Eds.), Indirect Demographic Estimation: Methods for Developing Countries (pp. 213–245). International Union for the Scientific Study of Population (IUSSP), United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the core entity ('Tools for Demographic Estimation') and the correct context (2013 publication). Agent B failed the Deep Logic completely, hallucinating a book title and incorrectly citing the author (T.A. Moultrie) in 1983, which is the year of the original UN Manual X, not the successor project. While Agent A missed a few editors in the citation, it is the only response that identified the correct work.
